stem: A agriculture study measures crop yield across 4 regions, each containing multiple farms. region A has 5 observations with mean 77.1 tonnes/hectare (within-region SD = 6.6). region B has 8 observations with mean 56.9 tonnes/hectare (within-region SD = 7.4). region C has 20 observations with mean 65.7 tonnes/hectare (within-region SD = 3.9). region D has 15 observations with mean 60.4 tonnes/hectare (within-region SD = 4.1). The between-region standard deviation is uncertain, estimated between 8.2 and 20.4 tonnes/hectare. Using a hierarchical Bayesian model, estimate the true crop yield for region D, accounting for shrinkage toward the grand mean and the uncertainty in the between-region variation.

ground_truth.answer: Hierarchical estimate for region D: 60.4 tonnes/hectare (shrunk from raw 60.4). Range [60.4, 60.5] as between-region SD varies from 8.2 to 20.4. Pooled mean: 63.8.

solution_steps: Compute pooled (grand) mean: 63.8 tonnes/hectare (weighted average of 4 region means).
Shrinkage factor for region D: B = σ²_between / (σ²_between + σ²_within/15). At σ_between=13.6: B = 0.994.
Interval [60.4, 60.5] captures uncertainty from the unknown between-group variance.
common_failure_modes: Using the raw observed mean (60.4) without shrinkage
Using only the grand mean (63.8) and ignoring group-specific data
Not recognizing that small-sample groups should be shrunk more toward the grand mean
Treating the between-group variance as known rather than uncertain
